Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

My only thought is if possible, it would be nice to put this in some client only area of /proc/.  I'm a bit worried about misleading people with the server side option.  Otherwise the patch looks good.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I agree with Patrick, this patch will make it, but it may puzzle users of the feature on Server! May be you can find a Client only place in /proc, or at least create+use a wrapper of proc_dointvec() to return error/log msg upon usage on Server?

Patch Set 4:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

Patchset 4 was just a rebase on master.
Patchset 5 made a slight change to move ptlrpc_invalidate_import() to after the LBUG. That way, if we have lbug_on_eviction, and panic_on_lbug set, we can preserve the state of the import at the time of eviction. This has been a lot more useful to us in debugging problems.

Patch Set 5:

Looks good to me.
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 5:

Response to some previous comments, including my own:
Servers can also get evicted, particularly in the case of DNE2.  This would likely be useful on the server side in that case, so the server vs client concern doesn't apply.  It's a good feature on both.

Also, I don't agree about wanting panic() instead of LBUG on eviction.  Panic on LBUG is on by default, and if it's off, we want exactly the behavior that results.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 7:

What are you trying to accomplish? Is this so nodes are forced to reboot after an eviction?

Patch Set 7:

We really just use in conjunction with panic_on_lbug for debugging purposes. We want dumps of clients when they are evicted so that we have better data on the state of the client at the time of eviction. There's more discussion on this in the LU ticket.

Removed the following votes:

* Code-Review+1 by Ryan Haasken <haasken@cray.com>

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+2

Ryan, I removed your +1 because I thought you shouldn't be marking your own patch +1, but I see it was later refreshed by Chris so that is OK to do in this case.  Please re-review.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Is this something you need right away? I really doubt this approach would be accepted upstream. This could be done with trace point trigger events. As an
example the following trigger dumps a stacktrace every time the kmalloc tracepoint is hit:

  # echo 'stacktrace' > \
        /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/kmem/kmalloc/trigger

 You can even do fancier things like trigger a stacktrace the first 5 times a kmalloc
  request happens with a size >= 64K

  # echo 'stacktrace:5 if bytes_req >= 65536' > \
        /sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/kmem/kmalloc/trigger

For lustre  could create a special evict event for you instead.
The only thing is you have to wait for about a month I would say
before tracepoint would land to master.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.subject: LU-5026 obdclass: Add lbug_on_eviction option
commit.message:
LU-5026 obdclass: Add lbug_on_eviction option

Add an lbug_on_eviction sysfs interface.  When it is set to a non-zero
value on a client, it will cause the client to LBUG whenever it is
evicted by the server. Note, an MDS is a client to OSTs, and every
server is a client of MGS. Thus, it is probably desireable to leave
this set to zero on servers.

Change-Id: I2f694e4bc1b1ec9daf52f8a6ac46ad87f40f810f
